title: PROP-FILTER-022 maps to wrong edge case ID (EC-S-001)
description:
verification-architecture.md line 116 declares:
  `PROP-FILTER-022 | Rapid keystrokes: SearchApplied dispatched only after last keystroke + 200ms silence | REQ-FILTER-002 / EC-S-001`

behavioral-spec.md §4.1 row 1 defines EC-S-001 as:
  `Empty string dispatched via SearchApplied → Reducer sets searchQuery: ''. No search predicate applied. All notes visible (subject to tag filter).`

The two are unrelated. PROP-FILTER-022 is about debounce coalescing of multiple keystrokes; EC-S-001 is about the empty-string dispatch path. The cross-reference therefore points the future test author at the wrong scenario. This is also a regression risk: a test asserting only EC-S-001 behavior would mark PROP-FILTER-022 'PASS' without ever exercising the rapid-keystroke debounce.

evidence.snippet: PROP-FILTER-022 | Rapid keystrokes: SearchApplied dispatched only after last keystroke + 200ms silence | REQ-FILTER-002 / EC-S-001 | 1 | true | vitest with fake timers

remediation: Either (a) introduce a new EC ID for 'rapid keystrokes coalesced into single SearchApplied' and reference it, or (b) repoint PROP-FILTER-022 to an existing edge case that genuinely covers rapid keystrokes (none currently does in §4.1; the closest is EC-S-005 in the inline definition, which is itself disputed — see FIND-SPEC-FILTER-012).
